name: Joe Benjamin
abstract: Joseph Rupert Benjamin (November 4, 1919 – January 26, 1974) was an American jazz bassist. Born in Atlantic City, New Jersey, Benjamin played with many jazz musicians in a variety of idioms.

concerts.label: Jazz Concert featuring Count Basie and His Orchestra, Charlie Parker, and Sarah Vaughan

about_quotes.speaker: John Malachi
about_quotes.evidence: So with these two guys, it turns out that I was in another happy situation as an accompanist to Sarah, as well as playing with some good guys as trio members.

about_quotes.speaker: Harry Edison
about_quotes.evidence: I had Elvin Jones, Tommy Flanagan, Jimmy Forrest, and Joe Benjamin. I had a real good swinging band too... I kept that band together for about six years.
about_quotes.doc_title: Harry Edison — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Patricia Willard
about_quotes.evidence: And of course Joe Benjamin, boy, that was -- he loved that, because he would get into these heated conversations... But it was a very enlightening experience also.

about_quotes.speaker: Terry Clark
about_quotes.evidence: So we formed a group, CTBB... One time we had Joe Benjamin on bass.
about_quotes.doc_title: Terry Clark — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: John Beal
about_quotes.evidence: I had to play with Duke Ellington’s band one time and it was a TV show. And I remember Joe Benjamin had a fender bender and I was on the beach at Fire Island... I went in and subbed that night on the TV show
about_quotes.doc_title: John Beal interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, June 18, 2024

about_quotes.speaker: Wilbur Ware
about_quotes.evidence: I was asking Joe Benjamin (PHONETIC) -- Joe Benjamin was going to play next with Clark Terry, so I asked him can I use his bass. He said, 'No, you should have brought yours.'
about_quotes.doc_title: 68088_Wilbur B. Ware, Sr oral history interview
